question_text: Describe the cultural significance of 'Ayodhya' on the basis of ancient literature.

points.text: Ayodhya, situated on the banks of the Sarayu river, holds a pre-eminent position in ancient Indian literature as one of the seven holiest cities (Saptapuri) and the capital of the Ikshvaku dynasty.

In the Valmiki Ramayana, it is described as an impregnable and prosperous city ('unconquerable') representing the ideal of 'Ramrajya,' characterized by righteousness, social harmony, and administrative excellence.
Ancient Puranic and Buddhist literature further highlight its significance as a center of diverse religious traditions, including being the birthplace of several Jain Tirthankaras.
Ayodhya remains a foundational pillar of Indian cultural identity, symbolizing the synthesis of spiritual values and the legendary heritage of the Suryavanshi kings.

question_text: Agra's Indo-Islamic architecture has blend of Indian, Persian and Islamic styles. Comment.

points.text: Agra served as the heart of Mughal architecture, where the fusion of diverse artistic traditions reached its zenith, creating a unique Indo-Islamic aesthetic.
Indian influences are visible in the use of red sandstone, trabeated construction elements, and decorative motifs like the lotus and kalash found in the Agra Fort and Fatehpur Sikri.
Persian and Islamic styles contributed the use of white marble, the 'charbagh' garden layout, bulbous domes, and intricate Pietra Dura work seen in the Taj Mahal and Itmad-ud-Daulah.
This synthesis not only redefined the skyline of Agra but also established a sophisticated architectural language that represented the inclusive cultural vision of the Mughal era.

question_text: Discuss in short the contribution of Arya Samaj in education of Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: Founded by Swami Dayananda Saraswati, the Arya Samaj played a transformative role in the educational awakening of Uttar Pradesh by blending Vedic values with modern scientific learning.
The establishment of the DAV (Dayanand Anglo-Vedic) network and Gurukuls (like Gurukul Kangri) provided an alternative to colonial education, emphasizing character building and nationalism.
The Samaj was a pioneer in promoting women's education and Sanskrit learning, breaking traditional barriers to make knowledge accessible to all sections of society.
The educational legacy of Arya Samaj continues to influence the academic landscape of the state, fostering a generation rooted in indigenous values and modern outlook.

question_text: Elucidate the cultural significance of literature and music in day to day life of people of Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: Literature and music are not merely art forms in Uttar Pradesh; they are deeply woven into the social fabric, marking every phase of life from birth to harvest.
Folk music genres like Birha, Kajari, and Chaiti reflect the seasonal cycles and daily struggles of the rural population, providing a sense of community identity and emotional outlet.
The rich literary tradition of Awadhi and Braj Bhasha, through the works of Tulsidas and Surdas, remains a living influence in daily prayers, idioms, and local festivities.
This vibrant oral and written tradition ensures the continuity of the state's cultural heritage, making art an inseparable part of the common man's existence.

question_text: Comment on the role of the Governor of Uttar Pradesh as a Constitutional Head.

points.text: The Governor of Uttar Pradesh acts as the nominal executive head, representing the President and ensuring that the state administration is carried out according to the Constitution.
Roles include appointing the Chief Minister, summoning the state legislature, and giving assent to bills, while acting on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers.
The Governor also exercises discretionary powers in specific situations, such as recommending President's Rule under Article 356 or reserving bills for the President's consideration.
The office serves as a vital constitutional link between the Center and the State, maintaining the federal balance and the rule of law within the province.

question_text: Discuss the Jurisdiction of Allahabad High Court and its role in upholding Constitutional values.

points.text: The Allahabad High Court, one of the oldest and largest in India, exercises original, appellate, and writ jurisdiction over the entire state of Uttar Pradesh.
Under Article 226, the court plays a pivotal role in protecting Fundamental Rights through the issuance of writs against the state's administrative actions.
Its landmark judgments on personal liberty, social justice, and executive accountability have consistently reinforced the 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution at the state level.
As a 'Court of Record,' it serves as the final guardian of democratic values in the state, ensuring that the government operates within its legal and moral boundaries.

question_text: Comment on the relevance of the Right to Information Act in Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: The RTI Act 2005 has become a powerful tool for good governance in Uttar Pradesh, empowering citizens to hold the vast state bureaucracy accountable.
It has enhanced transparency in the delivery of welfare schemes, public procurement, and recruitment processes, thereby acting as a significant deterrent to corruption.
The relevance is further amplified by the 'Jan-Sunwai' portal, which integrates digital transparency with the legal mandate of the RTI for faster grievance redressal.
Despite challenges like pendency, the RTI remains the cornerstone of citizen-centric administration, bridging the gap between the ruler and the ruled.

question_text: Define Social Media and identify few threats it has generated for internal security with special reference to Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: Social Media refers to interactive digital platforms that facilitate the creation and sharing of information and ideas through virtual communities.
In Uttar Pradesh, a major threat is the rapid spread of 'fake news' and misinformation, which can incite communal tensions and lead to law-and-order crises.
Other risks include cyber-radicalization of youth, the use of encrypted platforms for organized crime, and the spread of digital propaganda aimed at delegitimizing state institutions.
Managing these dual-edged platforms requires a robust state cyber-security framework and proactive 'community policing' in the digital sphere.

question_text: Underline the main challenges pertaining to health in rural areas of Uttar Pradesh in the light of latest rural health data.

points.text: While rural health infrastructure in Uttar Pradesh has expanded, latest data highlights persistent gaps in the quality and accessibility of medical services.
Primary challenges include the shortage of specialist doctors at Community Health Centers (CHCs) and inadequate primary healthcare facilities in remote villages.
Issues like high out-of-pocket expenditure, prevalence of malnutrition, and regional disparities in maternal and child health indicators continue to hinder rural well-being.
Strengthening rural health requires a focused approach on improving the doctor-patient ratio and the effective implementation of the Ayushman Bharat scheme.

question_text: Discuss innovation in the agricultural sector of Uttar Pradesh and analyze its impact on farmer's income and rural employment.

points.text: Innovation in UP's agriculture involves the adoption of digital tools, high-yield varieties, and modern post-harvest techniques to transform traditional farming.
Technologies like drip irrigation, soil health cards, and e-NAM have improved resource efficiency and market access, directly boosting farmers' net income.
The growth of food processing units and agritech startups in the state has created new avenues for rural employment, reducing the dependency on disguised labor in fields.
Continuous investment in 'Smart Agriculture' is essential to transition from subsistence farming to a sustainable and profitable agri-business model in the state.

question_text: "Ancient Mathura was the womb place of Jain Archaeological sources." Clarify this statement on the basis of Jain Archaeological sites in Braj Region.

points.text: Mathura, the heart of the Braj region, served as a vital center for Jainism for over a millennium, yielding a treasure trove of archaeological evidence that tracks the religion's evolution.

The Kankali Tila site is the most significant 'womb' of Jain archaeology, providing Ayagapatas (votive tablets), highly ornate pillars, and Tirthankara idols that date back to the Shunga and Kushana periods.
Archaeological findings in the region confirm that Mathura was the birthplace of unique Jain iconography and the site where the 'Saraswati Movement' for preserving Jain scriptures took shape.
These archaeological sources from the Braj region are indispensable for reconstructing the early history, art, and institutional growth of Jainism in Northern India.

question_text: Elucidate the significance of Magh Mela and Kumbh Mela in the culture of Uttar Pradesh and focus on their salient features.

points.text: The Magh Mela and the monumental Kumbh Mela held at Prayagraj represent the pinnacle of spiritual congregationalism and the living cultural heritage of Uttar Pradesh.
Magh Mela is an annual ritual held during the month of Magh, focused on 'Kalpavas' where devotees live a monastic life on the riverbanks to attain spiritual purification.

Kumbh Mela, occurring every 12 years, is recognized by UNESCO as intangible heritage, featuring the 'Shahi Snan' (royal bath) by various Akharas and attracting millions in a symbol of universal brotherhood.
These melas are not just religious events but massive socio-economic drivers that showcase the state's capacity for large-scale administrative and logistical management.

question_text: How did cities of Meerut, Lucknow, Kanpur and Jhansi in Uttar Pradesh contribute to the 1857 Revolt and what were the key events that took place in the State during this pivotal period?

points.text: Uttar Pradesh was the main theater of the 1857 First War of Independence, where regional centers coordinated a massive armed resistance against the British East India Company.

Meerut served as the spark of the revolt; Lucknow witnessed the intense Siege of the Residency under Begum Hazrat Mahal; Kanpur saw the leadership of Nana Saheb; and Jhansi became immortal through the valor of Rani Lakshmibai.
Key events included the declaration of Bahadur Shah Zafar as Emperor in Delhi by Meerut sepoys and the fierce street-to-street fighting in Awadh that briefly ended British control over the province.
The 1857 revolt in these cities laid the psychological foundation for the future Indian national movement and fundamentally changed British administrative policies in India.

question_text: Elucidate the significance of Birha and Kajari folk songs in the cultural life of Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: Birha and Kajari are foundational pillars of the Bhojpuri and Awadhi oral traditions, reflecting the emotional landscape and agricultural cycles of eastern Uttar Pradesh.
Birha, traditionally a narrative ballad of the 'Yadav' community, serves as a medium for social commentary and historical storytelling, often performed during late-night village gatherings.
Kajari is a seasonal song of the monsoon (Shravan), expressing the theme of longing and the beauty of nature, traditionally sung by women during festivities like Teej.
By documenting the joys and sorrows of rural life, these folk songs preserve the linguistic diversity and communal harmony of the state's vibrant cultural life.

question_text: Describe the Constitutional provisions related to Centre-State relations in Indian Constitution and throw light on its relevance in the context of Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: The Indian Constitution defines Centre-State relations through Legislative (Articles 245-255), Administrative (Articles 256-263), and Financial (Articles 264-293) frameworks.
Relevance in Uttar Pradesh is seen in 'Cooperative Federalism,' where the state aligns its massive developmental projects (like Expressways) with Central schemes (PM Gati Shakti).
Financial relations are critical for UP, as the state relies on Finance Commission devolutions to fund its social infrastructure while managing its large population needs.
A harmonious Centre-State relationship is essential for Uttar Pradesh to achieve its goal of becoming a 1-trillion dollar economy within the Indian federal structure.

question_text: Discuss the composition, powers and functions of the Legislative Assembly of Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: The Vidhan Sabha (Legislative Assembly) of Uttar Pradesh is the largest state legislative body in India, currently composed of 403 directly elected members.
Powers include the exclusive right to initiate Money Bills and the joint power with the Legislative Council to legislate on subjects in the State and Concurrent lists.
Key functions involve exercising control over the Executive through motions, questions, and the passing of the state budget to ensure administrative accountability.
As the primary representative body, the Assembly reflects the political aspirations of the state's diverse population and serves as the forge for provincial laws.

question_text: "Citizen Charter should be of the Citizen, by the Citizen and for the Citizen." Discuss with special reference to Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: A Citizen's Charter is a commitment by a government department to provide specific services within a set timeframe, aiming for a citizen-centric administration.
In Uttar Pradesh, this philosophy is implemented through the 'Jan-Sunwai' portal and the 'Right to Public Service Act,' which provide a legal mandate for the Charter's promises.
To be truly 'by and for the citizen,' departments must involve local stakeholders in designing charters and ensure that grievance redressal is swift and local.
Transforming these charters from mere documents to 'accountability tools' is vital for improving the 'Ease of Living' for the common man in the state.

question_text: Elucidate the relationship between organized crime and terrorism and examine its impact on security of Uttar Pradesh.

points.text: The nexus between organized crime and terrorism creates a 'hybrid threat' where criminal networks provide the logistics, funding, and hardware for terror activities.
In Uttar Pradesh, this relationship often manifests as illegal arms manufacturing, narcotics smuggling across borders, and 'Hawala' transactions used to fund extremist sleeper cells.
The impact on security includes the destabilization of communal harmony, the creation of a 'shadow economy,' and the erosion of the rule of law in sensitive districts.
Combating this requires a specialized approach like the Uttar Pradesh Special Task Force (STF) and the use of technology for real-time tracking of criminal-terrorist financial flows.

question_text: Evaluate the role of Uttar Pradesh Science and Technology Policy, 2025 and the Council of Science and Technology (CST-UP) in building a resilient R&D ecosystem.

points.text: The Science and Technology Policy 2025 aims to position Uttar Pradesh as a regional hub for innovation, with the CST-UP acting as the primary execution agency.
CST-UP builds the ecosystem by funding 'Deep-Tech' startups, establishing incubation centers in universities, and providing research grants for indigenous solutions in agriculture and health.
The policy prioritizes the commercialization of Lab-to-Land technologies and the development of a 'STEM' talent pool to attract high-value R&D investments to the state.
This forward-looking framework is essential for transitioning UP into a knowledge-based economy and ensuring long-term technological self-reliance.

question_text: Critically examine the socio-economic potential of the new UP AYUSH Policy, 2025 in promoting wellness tourism and agricultural diversification.

points.text: The UP AYUSH Policy 2025 leverages the state's traditional medical heritage to create a modern industry focused on holistic health and preventive wellness.
Socio-economically, it promotes wellness tourism by establishing AYUSH hubs in spiritual centers like Varanasi and Ayodhya, attracting global travelers seeking traditional treatments.
In agriculture, the policy incentivizes farmers to diversify into high-value medicinal and aromatic plant cultivation, supported by assured buy-back mechanisms and state processing units.
By integrating health with livelihood, the AYUSH policy serves as a dual tool for cultural preservation and sustainable rural development in Uttar Pradesh.
